zh.of, Walton, Lancashire, England: 英國藍卡爾沃爾頓 奧 馮 希爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Woodcock於大約1286年出生在英國藍卡爾沃爾頓 奧 馮 希爾時，他的父親William Richard Woodcock28歲，母親Woodcock24歲。他於大約1308年與Lady Richard Woodcock結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1308年在英國英格蘭享年23歲。

nameMeanings.name: Woodcock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Middle English wodecok ‘woodcock’ (Old English wuducocc, a compound of Old English wudu ‘wood’ + cocc ‘cock, bird’), a type of bird that is notoriously easy to catch. Therefore, the name might have been either for a person who caught or sold woodcock, or a nickname for a gullible person.

in some cases, because there can be confusion between -cock and -cot in surnames, and therefore the name may also be a variant of Woodcott, a habitational name from any of various places called with Old English wudu ‘wood’ + cot ‘cottage, shelter’, such as Woodcott in Cheshire and Hampshire or Woodcote in Hampshire, Surrey, Oxfordshire, Warwickshire, and Shropshire, or from any residence at a cottage in or near a wood.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Woodcock was born about 1286, in Walton on the Hill, Lancashire, England, his father, William Richard Woodcock, was 28 and his mother, Woodcock, was 24. He married Lady Richard Woodcock about 1308. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1308, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 23.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Woodcock(이)가 대략 1286년 영국 랭카실르 월턴  테 힐에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Richard Woodcock(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Woodcock(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1308년 Lady Richard Woodcock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1308년 영국 잉글랜드에서 23세의 나이로 사망했다.
